title: ReaPack
notes: Free package manager for REAPER. Installs thousands of community scripts/extensions in a couple clicks — this is the single highest-value free tool for a REAPER user that isn't already on your list.

title: VMPK (Virtual MIDI Piano Keyboard)
notes: Free, open source. Turns your computer keyboard/mouse into a MIDI controller when you don't have a physical one handy — useful for quick sketching on the road.

title: MidiEditor
notes: Free, open source standalone MIDI file editor/piano roll — handy for cleaning up or hand-editing MIDI files outside your DAW.

title: Captain Chords
notes: Paid ($79+) VST/AU chord & progression writer with genre-sorted presets, drag MIDI straight into your DAW. The industry go-to for fast chord ideas.

title: Scaler 2
notes: Paid, widely considered the standard scale/chord-detection and MIDI generation plugin. Detects the key/scale of anything you feed it and suggests chord progressions that fit.

title: Chordify
notes: Free (with paid tier). Paste any song link and it auto-detects the chord progression in real time as the track plays — great for quick reference or covers.

title: BandLab SongStarter
notes: Free AI-generated song starters (chords + drums + bass) as a jumping-off point when you're stuck, exportable as stems/MIDI.

title: Groove Monkee
notes: Free and paid MIDI drum loop packs across genres, built for dragging straight into a DAW to build realistic-feeling programmed drums fast.

title: Riffusion
notes: Weird one-off: real-time AI music generation from text prompts via spectrogram diffusion. Good for weird texture/sound-design starting points, not polished output.

title: 99Sounds
notes: Long-running free sample pack archive — drums, synths, foley, all genres, zero cost, no account needed.

title: Bedroom Producers Blog
notes: Long-running blog cataloguing free VST plugins, sample packs, and IRs as they're released. Good to check periodically.

title: MusicRadar Free Samples Archive
notes: A large, continuously updated hub of free royalty-free sample packs across genres from a major music-tech publication.

title: Voxengo SPAN
notes: Free, extremely capable real-time spectrum analyzer. A staple utility on nearly every professional's master bus.

title: LANDR Mastering
notes: AI mastering with a free tier for previews. Useful as a quick reference master to A/B against your own, even if you don't use its output as final.

title: Ownhammer
notes: Paid, extremely respected boutique cab IR developer — free samples/demos available per pack before buying.

title: Audacity
notes: Free, open source multi-track audio editor. The reliable fallback for quick edits, format conversion, or de-clicking/de-noising outside your main DAW.

title: MuseScore
notes: Free, open source notation software. Useful for writing out a quick lead sheet/chart for session players, or reading tab/notation.

title: CloudConvert
notes: Free-tier online file converter that handles virtually any audio/video/project format conversion in a browser, no install needed.

title: WhoSampled
notes: Free database tracing samples, covers, and remixes back to their sources — essential for hip-hop crate-digging research and clearance sanity checks.

title: RhymeZone
notes: Free rhyming dictionary and near-rhyme finder. Genuinely useful mid-writing-session tool that has nothing to do with plugins.

title: Chrome Music Lab
notes: Weird one-off: free, playful browser experiments (rhythm, spectrogram, oscillators) from Google. More toy than tool, but good for quick ear-training breaks or teaching kids the basics.
